## Summary

Describe the user-visible or governance problem and the bounded change.

## Source and authority

- [ ] New or changed third-party material is bound to an immutable revision.
- [ ] License, provenance, attribution, and redistribution limits are recorded.
- [ ] Official/runtime-owned material remains reference evidence unless an explicit admissible path exists.
- [ ] Skill bodies, suites, Hooks, adapters, tooling, and generated projections are classified separately.

## Verification

- [ ] Relevant verifier, unit tests, routing simulation, manifest check, topology check, and `git diff --check` pass.
- [ ] English and Chinese projections were updated together when applicable.
- [ ] Generated outputs were rebuilt from their authority source rather than hand-edited.
- [ ] Skipped checks and remaining evidence limits are stated.

## Safety and data review

- [ ] Candidate code, installers, Hooks, account connections, or external services were not executed unless separately authorized and evidenced.
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, private account state, personal memory, private configuration, restricted third-party content, or raw sensitive logs are included.
- [ ] Installation, runtime mutation, external writes, release, and consumer projection remain behind their applicable authority gates.

## Decision

State the disposition for each affected item: reference, hold, compare, adapt candidate, approve, reject, supersede, or retire. Explain why popularity, star count, suite membership, or local installation did not substitute for evidence.
